episodic.user_input: Addition is combining two or more numbers into one total. The answer to an addition is called the sum. The plus symbol means addition. Adding zero does not change a number. One plus one equals two. Two plus two equals four. Addition increases a number when adding a positive number. Addition is commutative. Changing the order of numbers does not change the sum. Three plus five equals five plus three. Addition is associative. Numbers can be grouped in any order when adding. Adding one increases a number by one. Adding two increases a number by two. Carry occurs when the sum of one column is ten or greater. Addition starts with the rightmost digit. Each column is added separately. A carry is added to the next column. The digits of the answer are written from right to left while calculating. The final carry becomes the leftmost digit of the answer. Ten ones equal one ten. Ten tens equal one hundred. Ten hundreds equal one thousand. Place value is important in addition. Ones are added to ones. Tens are added to tens. Hundreds are added to hundreds. Thousands are added to thousands. Every whole number has exactly one correct sum with another whole number. Adding a positive number always increases the total. Adding a negative number decreases the total. Adding the opposite of a number gives zero. Zero is the additive identity. Every number has an additive inverse. Addition is one of the four basic arithmetic operations. Large numbers are added using the same rules as small numbers. Decimals are added by lining up the decimal points. Fractions with the same denominator are added by adding the numerators.
